packTitle: Picnic Point Park — Engineering Site Review

modules.title: Ecology & Nearshore Habitat: Intertidal Beach, Forage Fish Spawning, Riparian Corridor, Marine Food Web
Restoration & Conservation: Healthy Forest Project, Olympic Fly Fishers Partnership, Shellfish Closure, Meadowdale Comparison
Geology & Coastal Processes: Glacial Bluff Sediments, Drift Cell Dynamics, BNSF Seawall Effects, USGS Landslide Mapping
History & Cultural Context: MV Pacific Queen, Shipwreck Point, BNSF Railroad, Tulalip Tribes Indigenous Shoreline
Recreation, Access & Community Science: Park Facilities, Tide-Dependent Beach Walks, City Nature Challenge Bioblitz, iNaturalist, MRC Forage Fish Protocol

bibliography: Snohomish County DCNR — Parks, Healthy Forest Project, Surface Water Management
Washington Department of Fish & Wildlife (WDFW) — Nearshore technical papers, shellfish closures, forage fish protocol
NOAA Fisheries (West Coast) — Chinook ESA listing, nearshore restoration research
Snohomish County Marine Resources Committee (MRC) — Forage fish spawning surveys since 2011 (700+ samples)
Puget Sound Maritime Historical Society (PSMHS) — MV Pacific Queen vessel identification and history
U.S. Geological Survey (USGS) — Landslide mapping, Seattle-Everett corridor
Olympic Fly Fishers of Edmonds (OFF) — Restoration event documentation (2023-2026)
iNaturalist / California Academy of Sciences — City Nature Challenge bioblitz platform
Washington Trails Association (WTA) — Trip reports, tide-dependent hiking conditions
WDFW Puget Sound Nearshore Technical Papers
safetyRules: SC-SRC: All numerical data attributed to named agency source with publication year
SC-NUM: No fabricated statistics — use documented or estimated with methodology disclosed
SC-END: No GPS coordinates for nesting bald eagles or other listed species (ABSOLUTE)
SC-IND: Tulalip Tribes content requires tribal consultation or published sources (GATE)
SC-ADV: Note permanent shellfish closure — no foraging guidance without WDFW/DOH confirmation
